skill.name: web-research
skill.description: Use this skill to plan, gather, and synthesize web research from multiple sources with clear citations.
skill.summary: Plan structured web research, collect focused sources, and produce cited findings.
skill.author: DougTrajano

security_audit.summary: The static weak cryptographic algorithm findings are false positives in normal Markdown prose, including description text and a note about no executable scripts. No executable code, prompt injection attempt, data exfiltration behavior, or unsafe tool instruction was found in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. The static finding at SKILL.md line 3 is prose in the frontmatter description for a web research methodology. It contains no cryptographic operation, weak algorithm selection, or executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is plain Markdown frontmatter describing the skill. There is no code path or cryptographic API usage to confirm the static pattern.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Duplicate Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. The duplicate static finding at SKILL.md line 3 refers to the same descriptive text. No additional security-relevant behavior is present on this line.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: This is a duplicate hit on the same prose-only line. The surrounding context confirms it is not cryptographic implementation code.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. The static finding at SKILL.md line 8 is a general statement about web research guidance. It does not define, recommend, or invoke a cryptographic algorithm.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is natural-language documentation about planning and synthesis. No executable syntax or security-sensitive operation is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. The static finding at SKILL.md line 10 is a note that the skill has no executable scripts or tools beyond the agent toolset. This reduces risk rather than indicating weak cryptography.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited note explicitly states the skill does not include executable scripts or tools. That context rules out a confirmed weak cryptographic implementation.

content.user_title: Research Web Topics With Cited Sources
content.value_statement: Open-ended web research can become scattered and hard to verify. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured process for planning searches, organizing sources, and synthesizing cited findings.
actual_capabilities: Breaks a research question into clear subtopics before searching.
Guides focused web searches with a suggested limit of three to five searches per subtopic.
Organizes findings while research is in progress.
Synthesizes collected information into a direct answer.
Prompts the assistant to cite source URLs and identify research gaps.
Supports simple fact-finding, comparative analysis, and broader investigations.
limitations: It is a methodology-only skill and does not add a browser or search API by itself.
Research quality depends on the available agent tools and accessible sources.
It does not verify source authenticity beyond the assistant's normal review process.
It does not perform continuous monitoring or automatic source updates.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Market Brief
use_cases.description: Compare vendors, trends, or customer segments and summarize the most relevant cited findings.

use_cases.title: Build a Source-Backed Article Outline
use_cases.description: Collect background information, identify gaps, and create a well-supported outline for writing.

use_cases.title: Investigate Technical Options
use_cases.description: Research libraries, tools, standards, or implementation approaches across multiple sources.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Topic Research
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when you need a short, cited overview of a topic.
prompt_templates.prompt: Research [topic]. Create a brief plan first, search for the key facts, then summarize the findings with source URLs and any gaps.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Several Options
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when choosing between products, tools, vendors, or approaches.
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare [option A], [option B], and [option C] for [decision context]. Use one subtopic per option, cite sources, and end with a balanced recommendation.

prompt_templates.title: Investigate a Complex Question
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when the question has multiple dimensions or uncertain evidence.
prompt_templates.prompt: Research [complex question]. Break it into three to five non-overlapping subtopics, gather evidence for each, synthesize the answer, and list unresolved questions.

prompt_templates.title: Produce a Research Report
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when you need a structured deliverable with evidence and limitations.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a research report on [subject] for [audience]. Include the research plan, findings by subtopic, source citations, synthesis, confidence level, and limitations.

output_examples.input: Research current project management tools for a small design agency.
output_examples.output: A concise comparison covering pricing, collaboration features, client access, and limitations, with source URLs and a short recommendation.

output_examples.input: Find evidence about adoption of passkeys in consumer applications.
output_examples.output: A structured brief with subtopics for adoption data, major platform support, user experience barriers, and remaining evidence gaps.

output_examples.input: Compare three JavaScript charting libraries for a dashboard project.
output_examples.output: A source-backed decision memo comparing features, licensing, performance notes, ecosystem maturity, and fit for the dashboard requirements.

best_practices: Start with a clear research question and define subtopics before searching.
Keep searches focused and stop when the evidence is sufficient for the decision.
Ask for source URLs, limitations, and confidence notes in the final answer.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for broad research without stating the decision or audience.
Do not treat one source as complete evidence for a complex topic.
Do not skip synthesis and return only a list of links.

faq.question: Does this skill browse the web by itself?
faq.answer: No. It provides a research process and depends on the web tools available to the assistant.

faq.question: Can it produce cited research reports?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill directs the assistant to synthesize findings and include source URLs.

faq.question: Is it useful for quick fact checks?
faq.answer: Yes. For simple facts, it recommends one or two focused subtopics instead of a large research plan.

faq.question: Can it compare multiple products or tools?
faq.answer: Yes. It suggests one subtopic per comparison item and a final synthesized answer.

faq.question: Does it include executable scripts?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed skill file states that it does not include executable scripts or extra tools.

faq.question: How should I get better results?
faq.answer: Provide the research question, target audience, decision context, and preferred depth before asking the assistant to search.
